text:
Market today: Cautious mood into quarter-end. Bulls are nervous about a pullback after a strong Q2 run (headline 6). The Cleveland Fed floating rate-HIKE language (headline 8) is the real threat to your book - higher rates hit high-multiple tech/chip names hardest, and you're on margin.

Your holdings: PTNM is down -15.25% and already close to its $9.50 stop - tiny spec on margin is the most urgent risk. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; moves can be extreme and margin amplifies every drop fast. SOXL is 3x leveraged semis - the rate-hike headline is a direct headwind; watch it closely against your $200 stop.

Watch: Cleveland Fed rate-hike rhetoric - if that gains traction, semis and high-multiple tech roll over fast. Also watch whether PTNM holds $9.50 or breaks it.

Bottom line: Your biggest job today is protecting capital on the two live grenades - PTNM and SOXL - before any new ideas matter.
plays: PTNM is -15.25% and hugging the $9.50 stop on margin - consider cutting it now or setting a hard stop at $9.50; no reason to let a tiny spec do more damage.
SOXL: rate-hike talk from the Fed is a direct gut-punch to 3x leveraged semis - tighten your mental stop toward $220-230 and be ready to act fast if the tone spreads.

No new buys today - scanner setups exist (AMAT 7/12, TXN 7/12) but with rate-hike headlines live and the market on edge after a big Q2, forcing a new leveraged-sector entry here is poor risk management.
